verdict_reasoning: SRC (c-Src) is canonically a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and a polybasic cluster — not the outer face. However, a 2026 Science study demonstrated that in cancer cells, Src undergoes topological inversion onto the extracellular face of the plasma membrane through autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), yielding extracellular membrane-associated Src (eSrc). This eSrc is present in primary tumors and is targetable by antibody-based therapeutics that mediate tumor cell killing. The protein reaches the outer face by its own (lipid-anchor-driven) mechanism after topological inversion — not via transient recruitment to another surface receptor. This cancer-cell-state-specific outer-face presentation qualifies as contextual.
